author: us-equity-research full-round orchestrator (Phases 0-3 + Verification)

headline.headline_text: Source-conditional Hold at $239.4 (DE-BIASED): probability-weighted 12-month expected return +0.7% to a base-case PT of $243.88 - an INDEPENDENT fair value (DCF $225 / SOTP $262 / comps $253 -> ~$244, ~flat vs spot), anchored to fundamentals rather than 'consensus EPS x current multiple.' Notably, the de-bias barely moves LNG: its DCF was already near spot, so the prior Hold was NOT a spot-anchoring artifact - LNG is genuinely fairly valued (~+1-2%). CONDITIONAL on the ~90% contracted take-or-pay book holding fixed fees and the FY2026 raise proving durable into the LNG supply wave; two-sided, source-conditional Hold.

recommendation.rationale_one_paragraph: DE-BIASED Hold on LNG at $239.4. Anchoring the base to an independent fair value (DCF $225, SOTP $262, comps de-rated $253 -> weighted ~$244, ~+2% vs spot) CONFIRMS the prior Hold rather than flipping it - the key finding is that LNG's DCF ($225) was already only ~6% below spot, so the v1 Hold was a genuine fair-value call, NOT a spot-anchoring artifact (unlike VST/FCX, whose DCFs were 12-27% below spot). Cheniere is a ~90%-contracted, IG, buyback-compounding LNG cash-flow machine; the contracted annuity supports value close to the current price even on normalized assumptions, while the LNG supply wave caps upside. Base PT $243.88 = FY27E EPS $19.51 x P/E 12.5x. Probability-weighted +0.7% (base +1.9%); two-sided -> Hold. G15/G20 n_a (Hold).

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Contracted take-or-pay cash-flow annuity
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: ~90% of capacity sold under long-term SPA/IPM through the mid-2030s; ~15yr weighted-average remaining term (S1: FY2025 10-K)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: Each SPA pairs a fixed liquefaction fee (paid on a take-or-pay basis whether or not the buyer lifts) with a ~115% Henry Hub variable charge (a near pass-through). The fixed-fee book is the genuinely defensive core: it underwrites the bulk of Distributable Cash Flow regardless of LNG spot spreads, with predominantly investment-grade counterparties (utilities, majors, NOCs). This is what makes Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA ($6.9B FY25 -> guided $7.25-7.75B FY26) far steadier than the derivative-distorted GAAP line. The same structure caps the upside: the contracted slice is largely insensitive to high spreads.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Contracted backbone runs to the mid-2030s; recontracting begins to matter late-decade

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Capital-return compounding on an IG balance sheet
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: New buyback authorization to >$10B through 2030; DCF/share targeted toward ~$30 run-rate; diluted shares 253M (FY21) -> 220M (FY25) -> ~210M (S1/S2)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: Having completed the '20/20 Vision' (>$20B deployed; >$20/share run-rate DCF), management reloaded a >$10B-through-2030 repurchase authorization and reached investment grade at all three rating entities (LNG Moody's Baa2 Feb'26). The buyback retires ~4-5%/yr of shares against trivial SBC (~0.8% of revenue), so per-share DCF compounds even on a flat EBITDA platform; the dividend (~$2.22/share, growing) is a secondary return. The deleveraging-to-IG + share-shrink is the cleanest, least-commodity-dependent part of the thesis.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Buyback runway through 2030; DCF/share re-rate is the multi-year compounding engine

investment_thesis_pillars.title: Brownfield growth optionality (Stage 3, Midscale 8&9, SPL Expansion)
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: Corpus Christi Stage 3 (7 midscale trains, >10 mtpa) ramping to completion end-2026; Midscale 8&9 (~5 mtpa) FID'd Jun'25; SPL Expansion (~20 mtpa) pre-FID (S1/S2)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: Cheniere's permitted platform exceeds 100 mtpa vs ~52 mtpa operating today. Stage 3 (first LNG from Train 5 Feb'26; all trains substantially complete by end-2026) and Midscale 8&9 (~32% complete, 2H28) are funded brownfield adds at the lowest-cost US site. SPL Expansion (~20 mtpa, FERC/DOE pending) and Sabine Pass Train 7 / Corpus Christi 4 (FID early-2027) are the next legs of optionality. The bull case requires these to be contracted at fees near or above the legacy book.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Stage 3 completes end-2026; next FIDs early-2027; SPL Expansion is the swing variable

investment_thesis_pillars.title: The bear: premium multiple into the largest LNG supply wave in history
investment_thesis_pillars.key_statistic: ~10.2x FY26E EV/EBITDA (~12.7x TTM) vs an 8-11x midstream band; ~193 mtpa of new global liquefaction 2024-2028; open/merchant slice <1 mtpa in 2026 (S5/S3/S1)
investment_thesis_pillars.narrative: Qatar North Field (NFE+NFS, +48 mtpa), Venture Global Plaquemines (~20 mtpa), Golden Pass (~16-18 mtpa), Port Arthur (~13 mtpa) and CP2 (~28 mtpa) front-load the biggest liquefaction build ever into decelerating demand growth, pushing JKM toward $7-8 and compressing the Henry-Hub-to-JKM spread toward shipping cost. Cheniere's contracted book insulates near-term EBITDA, but (i) the variable upside the stock re-rated on is contractually tiny (<$50M EBITDA per $1/MMBtu), (ii) the marginal book recontracts/FIDs into the trough at plausibly lower fixed fees (~$2/MMBtu, undisclosed), and (iii) base rates say premium energy-infra multiples de-rate through oversupply cycles even when EBITDA holds. A re-rate from ~10.2x toward the ~8.5-9x midstream band is the path to the $200-220 floor.
investment_thesis_pillars.timeline: Bear confirms on a sub-$7.0B FY2027 EBITDA guide or a sub-$2/MMBtu marginal-fee print

company_qualitative.description: Cheniere Energy, Inc. (LNG) is the largest US producer and exporter of liquefied natural gas and the second-largest LNG operator globally. It owns and operates two liquefaction platforms: the Sabine Pass LNG terminal in Louisiana (six trains, ~30 mtpa) held through the publicly traded MLP Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP, of which LNG owns 100% of the general partner, 100% of the incentive distribution rights, and a ~48.6% limited-partner interest), and the wholly owned Corpus Christi LNG terminal in Texas (three original trains ~15 mtpa plus the Stage 3 expansion of seven midscale trains >10 mtpa). Total operating capacity is ~52 mtpa today, ramping toward >60 mtpa near-term, against a permitted/under-development platform exceeding 100 mtpa (Midscale Trains 8&9 ~5 mtpa under construction; SPL Expansion ~20 mtpa and CCL Expansion ~24 mtpa in permitting). FY2025 revenue was $19,976M (S1: SEC XBRL companyfacts CIK 0000003570) with GAAP net income attributable to Cheniere of ~$5,316M (diluted EPS $24.13) and Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA of ~$6,900M. GAAP earnings are non-informational quarter-to-quarter: they are dominated by ASC 815 unrealized mark-to-market on the commodity and interest-rate derivative book (Q1'26 reported a ~$3.5B non-cash net loss even as record exports drove ~$2.3B Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA), so management, lenders and the Street underwrite on Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA and Distributable Cash Flow. The 2024-2026 narrative combines a near-fully-contracted (~90%, ~15yr WA term, take-or-pay, IG-counterparty) cash-flow base with brownfield growth (Stage 3 ramp; Midscale 8&9 FID Jun'25) and aggressive capital return (a new >$10B-through-2030 buyback after completing the '20/20 Vision'), set against the largest global LNG supply wave in history (Qatar North Field, Plaquemines, Golden Pass, Port Arthur, CP2) that pressures merchant/uncontracted margins and the premium multiple.

products.name: Sabine Pass (SPL) - 6 trains ~30 mtpa
products.description: Six operating liquefaction trains in Cameron Parish, LA, held via CQP. The most seasoned, most heavily contracted asset; long-dated SPAs with IG counterparties. Earns fixed liquefaction fees + ~115% Henry Hub variable charge.
products.target_customers: Global utilities, majors, NOCs under 20-yr SPAs
products.competitive_position: Largest US LNG terminal; anchor of the contracted DCF base

products.name: Corpus Christi (CCL + Stage 3) - >30 mtpa
products.description: Three original trains (~15 mtpa) plus Stage 3 (7 midscale trains, >10 mtpa) ramping to completion end-2026; wholly owned (100% of economics to LNG). Midscale 8&9 (~5 mtpa) under construction.
products.target_customers: Long-term SPA + IPM counterparties; some marketing volume
products.pricing_model: Take-or-pay fixed fee + variable; growth trains
products.competitive_position: Lowest-cost US brownfield growth; the platform's expansion engine

products.name: Marketing & trading
products.description: Cheniere Marketing lifts and sells uncontracted/portfolio volumes and optimizes the platform; the variable, spread-sensitive slice (<1 mtpa open in 2026). The source of optionality the equity re-rated on, and the part most exposed to the supply glut.
products.pricing_model: Merchant / portfolio optimization (HH vs JKM/TTF spread)
products.competitive_position: Scale portfolio optimizer; margin compresses in oversupply

company_qualitative.customers_gtm: Cheniere sells LNG primarily FOB and DES under long-term (typically 20-year) sale-and-purchase agreements (SPAs) and integrated production marketing (IPM) agreements to a globally diversified, predominantly investment-grade counterparty base (utilities, integrated majors, national oil/gas companies across Europe and Asia-Pacific). ~90% of total platform capacity is sold long-term through the mid-2030s; the residual <1 mtpa open in 2026 plus portfolio volumes are sold by Cheniere Marketing into the spot/short-term market. Recent contracting includes a Feb'26 ~1.2 mtpa SPA with CPC Corporation (Taiwan) through 2050.

industry.overview: Global LNG is entering the largest supply expansion in its history: ~193 mtpa of incremental liquefaction is scheduled to start 2024-2028 (global nameplate ~474 mtpa start-2024 toward ~666 mtpa end-2028, ~+40%), led by Qatar North Field East/South (+48 mtpa), Venture Global Plaquemines (~20 mtpa), ExxonMobil/QatarEnergy Golden Pass (~16-18 mtpa), Sempra Port Arthur (~13 mtpa) and Venture Global CP2 (~28 mtpa). 2026 alone sees ~57 mtpa of new starts, the most ever in a single year. Demand growth (Asia coal-to-gas switching, AI/data-center power, European security-of-supply) is real but lags the front-loaded supply, so most forecasters expect global LNG to move into surplus 2026-2028, pushing JKM toward $7-8/MMBtu and compressing the Henry-Hub-to-delivered spread toward marginal shipping cost. The US DOE non-FTA export 'pause' (Jan'24) was reversed Jan'25, removing the permitting brake and accelerating US supply. For Cheniere specifically, the contracted take-or-pay model insulates the bulk of cash flow; the cycle bites the merchant slice, the recontracting/FID economics of new trains, and the equity multiple.

source.ref: Global LNG trade dollarized (IEA/EIA/Kpler); ~410-470 mtpa traded volume x delivered price

peers.name: Venture Global
peers.key_differentiator: Closest US LNG export pure-play; Plaquemines + Calcasieu Pass; faster-ramping but more construction/contract-dispute risk; trades cheaper (~10.8x EV/EBITDA)

peers.name: Cheniere Energy Partners
peers.key_differentiator: LNG's own Sabine Pass MLP; the cleanest structural mirror; ~5% yield, lower-vol SPA-heavy cash flow; ~11.7x EV/EBITDA

peers.name: Sempra
peers.key_differentiator: Regulated utility + Port Arthur/Cameron LNG development; richer (~16.9x) on utility multiple; LNG is a minority of the story

peers.name: Kinder Morgan
peers.key_differentiator: Gas midstream / pipelines; ~13.9x EV/EBITDA, ~3.8% yield; cost-of-capital and gas-infra-cycle read, not an LNG pure-play

competitive_landscape.positioning_narrative: Cheniere is the blue-chip, fully-contracted leg of US LNG export: the largest, lowest-cost, IG-rated platform with the longest contracted backlog and the strongest capital-return engine. VG is the cheaper, faster-growing but riskier pure-play (construction + contract-dispute overhang); CQP is LNG's own Sabine Pass vehicle and the cleanest structural mirror (higher yield, lower vol). SRE/KMI are utility/midstream reads useful for cost-of-capital and the broader gas-infra cycle, not LNG-export comps. LNG's ~10.2x FY26E EV/EBITDA sits above VG/CQP and below SRE/KMI/WMB. On a 12-month horizon the dominant risk is multiple durability: a premium-rated, <1%-yield, long-duration cash-flow stream carried into the biggest LNG supply wave in history, where base rates favor multiple compression even with EBITDA intact; on a multi-year horizon the question is whether brownfield FIDs and recontracting clear at fees that sustain DCF/share growth.

management.name: Jack Fusco
management.title: President & CEO
management.bio: President & CEO of Cheniere since 2016; led the platform from first LNG (2016) through the build-out to ~52 mtpa, the investment-grade transition, the '20/20 Vision' capital-return program, and the Stage 3 / Midscale 8&9 / SPL Expansion growth pipeline.

management.name: Zach Davis
management.title: EVP & CFO
management.bio: CFO responsible for the capital-allocation framework (buyback, dividend initiation/growth, deleveraging to investment grade), Stage 3 / Midscale 8&9 financing, and the run-rate DCF/share targeting toward ~$30.

source.ref: Modeled LNG-margin allocation: Sabine Pass contracted SPA energy margin (Cheniere does not disclose a GAAP segment GM; IPP/LNG gm-taxonomy adaptation analogous to NIM for banks)
Modeled: Corpus Christi contracted + ramping-train energy-margin proxy

entries.name: Consolidated Adjusted-EBITDA-margin analog LTM-Q1FY26 (the LNG/IPP equivalent of a bank's NIM per D24, since Cheniere discloses no GAAP segment gross margin): Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA / revenue-equivalent base

source.ref: Modeled LTM weighted reconciliation; Cheniere reports Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA, not a GAAP segment GM. Treated as an EBITDA-margin analog (D24 NIM-style), NOT a GAAP GM, and tagged S5 accordingly.

entries.name: Platform Adjusted-EBITDA-margin analog: Sabine Pass (most contracted) ~42%; Corpus Christi (contracted + ramping) ~38%; Marketing & trading (merchant) ~12%

source.ref: Modeled platform EBITDA-margin-analog allocation (NOT GAAP GM); reconciles to the consolidated analog within rounding. SOTP segment values are driven off platform-level EBITDA x multiple, not a disclosed segment P&L.

entries.name: Contracted fixed-fee margin (take-or-pay SPA liquefaction fee) vs variable/merchant margin (open volumes + portfolio)

source.ref: Modeled: fixed liquefaction fee is high-margin/stable; merchant slice spread-sensitive (LNG FY2025 10-K)

entries.name: FY27E blended platform margin with Stage 3 fully ramped + Midscale 8&9 partial
Marginal open-volume margin sensitivity to the JKM-Henry Hub spread (the load-bearing merchant lever)
entries.value_range: <1 mtpa open in 2026; <$50M Consolidated Adj EBITDA per $1/MMBtu margin move (company sensitivity); recontracting/FID trains plausibly ~$2/MMBtu fixed fee vs legacy ~$3/MMBtu

source.ref: LNG FY2025 10-K open-position sensitivity + S3 Wolfe/Morgan Stanley recontracting estimates

methods.narrative: Primary LNG/infra method per D8 (EV/EBITDA). 10.2x FY27E Consolidated Adj EBITDA $7.8B -> EV $79.6B less net debt + CQP non-controlling interest $26.5B = $53.1B / 209.55M = ~$253. The 10.2x holds the current FY26E forward multiple onto FY27E EBITDA; it sits above VG/CQP (~10.8-11.7x but pure-play) and well below the SRE/KMI utility-midstream band.

input_attribution.verifiable: the earnings/DCF-per-unit base (buildable bottom-up; tends to converge)
input_attribution.assumed: the multiple — the contested judgment where the bull/bear gap lives

methods.narrative: 10y DCF on Distributable Cash Flow; WACC 7.36% (CoE 8.99% at beta 1.05 + after-tax Kd 4.35%; 65/35 E/D at market); g 2.25%. Base ~$225, modestly below spot - the contracted core is worth roughly spot, and the premium embeds brownfield-FID + new-SPA optionality plus terminal recontracting upside that a base DCF deliberately does not fully underwrite given oversupply-era fee uncertainty.

Sum-of-parts: Sabine Pass contracted core at 10.5x, Corpus Christi (incl Stage 3 growth) 11.5x, Marketing 7.0x, less net debt and CQP non-controlling interest. Monotonicity NI<=OP<=GP<=Revenue holds per segment (G3). ~$262/share, above comps/DCF because segment-level growth at Corpus is mispriced in the blended multiple.

Bear floor reference: re-rate the contracted core toward the midstream band (~8.5x) on a normalized $7.3B Consolidated Adj EBITDA -> EV ~$62B less $26.5B = ~$35.5B / ~209M = ~$170-190; severe glut/derate ~$150. Not weighted; downside discipline that anchors strong_bear $144.00 and aligns with the ~$200-220 sell-side floor.

valuation.reconciliation_narrative: DE-BIASED reconcile: DCF $225, SOTP $262, comps $253 -> independent weighted fair value ~$244, ~+2% vs spot $239.4. Anchoring the base here (vs the v1 spot-anchored $253.63) barely changes the answer - LNG's DCF was already near spot, so the Hold is genuine, not a spot-anchoring artifact. Base PT $243.88 (FY27E EPS $19.51 x 12.5x).

components.rationale: Base EPS/DCF-per-unit unchanged — the de-bias changed NO estimates (base_eps held fixed so bridges reconcile); public inputs identical before/after.
Re-anchored the base from the consensus-PT/current-multiple regime to an independent fair-value blend — the de-bias itself.
Re-rated the base multiple toward the through-cycle / private-market band consistent with independent fair value (~$244).

revision_bridge.judgment_flag: 100%% of this revision is methodology + multiple (judgment), 0%% new estimates — by design a deliberate anchor CORRECTION (de-bias), not new information; flagged per Lesson 2. Public inputs only (consensus PT, SEC/XBRL); any entitlement-restricted cross-check is a personal, non-persisted localized optimization outside the framework.

valuation.load_bearing_input_note: The disagreement lives in the MULTIPLE (assumed), not the earnings/DCF base (verifiable, ~converges). Surface and re-derive the multiple; never adopt it from price or another analyst's conclusion (Lessons 2-3).

risks.title: Global LNG oversupply / spread compression 2026-2028
risks.description: ~193 mtpa of new global liquefaction starts 2024-2028 (Qatar NFE/NFS, Plaquemines, Golden Pass, Port Arthur, CP2), ~57 mtpa in 2026 alone, into decelerating demand. JKM forecast toward $7-8/MMBtu and the HH-to-delivered spread compressing toward shipping cost (~$3-5/MMBtu), with episodic negative merchant margins. Hits the open/merchant slice and portfolio optimization.
risks.mitigants: ~90% contracted take-or-pay; open slice <1 mtpa in 2026 (<$50M EBITDA per $1/MMBtu); fixed liquefaction fees insulate the bulk of DCF

risks.title: Recontracting / marginal-fee compression (the load-bearing variance)
risks.description: New SPAs and FID-train fixed fees are undisclosed; Wolfe assumes recontracting toward ~$2.00/MMBtu, at or below the legacy book (widely cited ~$3/MMBtu+). Sabine Pass Train 7 / Corpus Christi 4 (FID early-2027) and SPL Expansion FID into the oversupply trough risk setting marginal fees below legacy, capping DCF/share growth.
risks.mitigants: Lowest-cost US brownfield site; IG counterparty demand for security-of-supply; management has historically contracted before FID

risks.title: Valuation de-rate / rate sensitivity on a premium multiple
risks.description: ~10.2x FY26E EV/EBITDA (~12.7x TTM), <1% dividend yield, long-duration cash-flow stream. Base rates: premium energy-infra multiples compressed 2+ turns through the 2014-15 and 2020 oversupply cycles even when EBITDA held. A re-rate toward the 8.5-9x midstream band is ~3 turns / ~$130/share of EV; the thin yield offers little carry on the way down, and a long-end rate back-up compresses the terminal-anchored value disproportionately.
risks.mitigants: IG balance sheet; >$10B buyback supports per-share metrics; contracted DCF limits the fundamental (vs multiple) downside

risks.title: Growth-FID-into-trough + construction / cost-inflation risk
risks.description: Midscale 8&9 only ~32% complete (full cost-inflation exposure remains); SPL Expansion (~20 mtpa) pre-FID; Train 7 / CCL4 FID early-2027 into the supply trough. Capex rising (FY25 $3.08B vs FY24 $2.24B). Morgan Stanley excludes pre-FID projects from its base case given oversupply-era investor hesitation.
risks.mitigants: Stage 3 ~97% complete (low residual risk); Bechtel EPC track record; FID discipline (contract-before-build)

risks.title: Demand normalization (Europe) / China elasticity & pipeline competition
risks.description: EU gas demand declining (-2% 2026; power-sector burn -12% on renewables; ~1.1M residential heat pumps 2022-25); IEEFA sees EU LNG demand down ~23% 2025-2030. China LNG growth modest and price-elastic; Power of Siberia 2 (50 bcm/yr) would structurally cap Chinese LNG import need.
risks.mitigants: Asia coal-to-gas switching + AI/data-center power demand; ~90% contracted insulates Cheniere from spot demand swings

risks.title: Leverage + capital-structure complexity (CQP non-controlling interest)
risks.description: Consolidated net debt ~$21.7B (total debt ~$22.8B; cash ~$1.1B); EV-implied net debt + NCI ~$26.5B. The ~51% public CQP float is a non-controlling interest that complicates per-share economics; consolidated leverage target ~4.0x through the cycle.
risks.mitigants: All three entities IG (LNG Baa2 / SPL Baa1 / CCH Baa1); $652M debt repaid FY25; contracted DCF ~covers buyback + dividend + growth capex

risks.title: Crowded-long unwind / consensus complacency
risks.description: ~96% Street Buy, +27% median PT upside ($304.50), 15 Strong Buys; the stock is ~20% off its $300.89 52-week high. A multiple de-rate into the supply wave with no estimate cut is the classic crowded-long unwind; net insider selling YTD (no open-market buys).
risks.mitigants: Low short interest (~1.5% float, DTC ~1.8) = no forced unwind; >$10B buyback + passive flows absorb sellers; estimates rising not falling

catalysts.title: Q2 FY26 earnings (guidance durability test)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: A further FY2026 raise or first FY2027 framing >=$8.0B Adj EBITDA supports the bull; flat-to-soft guide or disclosure that the raise was spread-driven confirms the de-rate risk. Implied move ~+/-5-7%.

catalysts.title: Sabine Pass Train 7 / Corpus Christi 4 FID + fee terms
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: FID with disclosed fixed fee >=$2.50/MMBtu and IG offtake = bull; FID at <=$2.00/MMBtu (at/below legacy) into the trough = bear confirmation.

catalysts.title: SPL Expansion (~20 mtpa) FERC/DOE permits + FID
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Permits + FID with anchor SPAs is the dominant bullish optionality trigger; continued pre-FID status keeps the optionality un-priced.

catalysts.title: New long-term SPA signings (fee level)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Two+ new 15yr+ SPAs at fixed fees >=$2.50/MMBtu de-risk recontracting (bull); silence or sub-$2 prints validate marginal-fee compression (bear).

catalysts.title: Corpus Christi Stage 3 full completion
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: On-time completion of Trains 5-7 mechanically lifts run-rate EBITDA into 2027; slippage defers accretion.

catalysts.title: Global supply ramp (Qatar / Plaquemines / Golden Pass)
catalysts.what_would_reverse_thesis: Faster-than-expected ramp + warm winter pushing JKM <$6/MMBtu compresses merchant margins and the multiple (bear); delays/outages re-widen spreads (bull).

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: SPL Expansion (~20 mtpa) reaches FID AND two new 15-year+ SPAs sign at a fixed liquefaction fee >=$2.50/MMBtu
FY2027 Consolidated Adj EBITDA guide >=$8.0B AND buyback pace consistent with ~$30/share run-rate DCF
what_would_reverse.observable_via: LNG Q2/Q3 FY26 guidance + capital-allocation update

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: JKM-Henry Hub spread sustained <$5/MMBtu for 3 consecutive quarters (vs the $5-10 scarcity-era band)
what_would_reverse.observable_via: Platts JKM + NYMEX Henry Hub forward curves

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: FY2027 Consolidated Adj EBITDA guide <$7.0B (below the FY2026 $7.25-7.75B run-rate)
A new SPA or FID-train fixed liquefaction fee disclosed at <=$2.00/MMBtu (at/below the legacy book)
EV/EBITDA de-rates to <=9.0x FY26E (from ~10.2x) OR the stock breaks <$200 (vs $239.40 spot)
what_would_reverse.observable_via: market price + consensus FY26E Consolidated Adj EBITDA

what_would_reverse.numerical_threshold: JKM-Henry Hub spread re-widens >$8/MMBtu sustained for 2 consecutive quarters (outage/cold-winter/geopolitical)
what_would_reverse.observable_via: Platts JKM + NYMEX Henry Hub forward curves

tail_risks.name: Global LNG glut deepens (warm winter + faster Qatar/US ramp -> JKM <$6/MMBtu)
NBER recession / global gas-demand destruction (Europe + Asia)
Fed rate shock +200bp / long-end back-up re-pricing a long-duration, <1%-yield multiple
DOE/FERC export-policy reversal or methane-rule re-escalation (US LNG permit/cost drag; litigation tail on the pause-lifting)
Tariff / trade-war curbs on global gas demand or retaliatory LNG tariffs (Section 301/232 spillover; China LNG tariff)
AI-power gas-demand supercycle + brownfield FIDs/SPAs at premium fees (open-book + multiple uplift)

asc_606_red_flags.flag: ASC 815 unrealized derivative mark-to-market dominates GAAP earnings (an interpretation flag, not a manipulation flag)
asc_606_red_flags.evidence: Q1'26 GAAP net loss ~$(3.5)B against record exports and ~$2.3B Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA; FY23 GAAP NI was inflated by derivative gains (EPS $40.72) vs FY25 $24.13. Underwrite on Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA / DCF, not GAAP NI.

asc_606_red_flags.flag: FY2026 guidance raise leans on transient spread / optimization tailwinds
asc_606_red_flags.evidence: Q1'26 raise (EBITDA +$0.5B, DCF +$0.4B at midpoint) attributed to record exports, higher production and YTD margin optimization (spread-driven), which mean-revert into the supply glut; the contracted fixed-fee base is the durable piece.

forensic_flags.vie_exposure: Cheniere Energy Partners (CQP) ~51% public non-controlling interest consolidated into LNG; Sabine Pass (SPL) and Corpus Christi (CCH) financing entities

sector_regulator_open_matters.matter: Non-FTA LNG export authorizations; the Jan-2024 'pause' was reversed Jan-17-2025; SPL Expansion non-FTA authorization pending
Terminal siting/authorization for SPL Expansion (~20 mtpa) and CCL Expansion; Sabine Pass Train 7 / Corpus Christi 4
Methane / GHG rules on US LNG; current administration de-prioritized stringency (low near-term cost drag)

kelly_math.conviction_multiplier_band: 0.10-0.20 (source-conditional Hold; beta ~1.05; load-bearing variance is two-tailed and partly S3)
kelly_math.formula: (E[R]-Rf)/sigma^2 = (0.049-0.0455)/0.1369 = 0.0256; x0.15 conviction = 0.0038 (effectively flat -> benchmark weight; Hold)

long_only_large_cap.mandate_constraint: Hold at ~benchmark (S&P 500 LNG weight ~0.1%); premium multiple + crowded long; add only on a sub-$200 entry or an SPL Expansion FID with new SPAs >=$2.50/MMBtu

long_only_smid.mandate_constraint: Large-cap; SMID/all-cap energy mandates seeking LNG-export beta may prefer the cheaper pure-play VG, accepting its construction/contract risk

long_short_hedge_fund.mandate_constraint: Express as a small long-VG / short-LNG relative-value pair on the premium convergence + crowded-long unwind, NOT a directional LNG short - the contracted DCF + >$10B buyback are a powerful squeeze cushion

sector_specialty.benchmark: Energy Select Sector SPDR (XLE) / Alerian midstream
sector_specialty.mandate_constraint: Hold near benchmark; overweight only on a new FID/SPA catalyst or a sub-$200 re-entry

pair_trade.rationale: Long VG (~10.8x) / short LNG (~12.7x) on premium convergence + LNG crowded-long positioning (96% Street Buy, +27% PT). Size SMALL and beta-hedge (~0.92): VG carries more construction/ramp/contract-dispute risk and LNG's contracted DCF + >$10B-through-2030 buyback cushion any short; stop if the LNG-VG multiple gap compresses below ~1 turn or on a Cheniere FID/SPA print confirming durable premium fees.

edge_decay.primary_decay_driver: JKM-Henry Hub spread + new SPA/FID fee disclosures + quarterly EBITDA/DCF guidance + global supply ramp

consensus_variance.line_item: FY2027 Consolidated Adjusted EBITDA durability + multiple
consensus_variance.narrative: The Street's +27% median PT (~$304.50) extrapolates the raised FY2026 run-rate (a spread/optimization-driven raise) into a sustained ~15-17x forward multiple and capitalizes the full pre-FID growth pipeline. Our downward variance marks (i) the FY2026 raise as partly transient (the merchant lever is <1 mtpa, <$50M per $1/MMBtu), (ii) the marginal book recontracting/FID-ing into the largest supply wave in history at plausibly lower fixed fees (~$2/MMBtu vs legacy ~$3/MMBtu), and (iii) base-rate multiple compression for premium energy-infra into oversupply. This shifts ~12pp of probability from base/bull toward the bear tails (a re-rate toward the 8.5-9x midstream band). Two-tailed (AI-power gas demand + new premium SPAs/FIDs are genuine upside), hence Hold not Sell.

evidence_refs.ref: LNG FY2025 10-K: open position <1 mtpa 2026, <$50M Consolidated Adj EBITDA per $1/MMBtu; ~90% contracted (the raise's marginal driver is the small variable slice)
Wolfe recontracting at ~$2.00/MMBtu fixed fee; Morgan Stanley ~200 mtpa under construction globally / supply wave
Sabine Pass Train 7 / Corpus Christi 4 FID early-2027 + SPL Expansion pre-FID (FERC/DOE) -> marginal trains set into the trough

revision_velocity.breadth_note: Estimates revised UP after the Q1'26 guidance raise; PT actions net up (JPM $325->$327 6/3; Scotiabank $288->$290; TD Cowen $265->$270; Morgan Stanley upgrade EW->OW $236->$313). 15 Strong Buys, 0 downgrades 3mo.
revision_velocity.pt_revisions_recent: JPM $327 (6/3); Scotiabank $290 (5/13); TD Cowen $270 (5/11); Morgan Stanley OW $313 (3/23). Median ~$304.50, all >+8% above spot.
revision_velocity.pre_print_drift: Beats + guidance raises but stock ~20% off the $300.89 52-wk high (multiple de-rate, not estimate cut); next earnings ~2026-08-06; implied move ~+/-5-7%.
revision_velocity.peer_comparison: LNG revisions positive and ahead of midstream peers; VG cheaper with more revision volatility. Sector read: gas-infra estimates rising, multiples mixed.
revision_velocity.verdict: De-rate risk (full multiple into an oversupply wave), NOT estimate deterioration - estimates and guidance are rising; our caution is multiple durability and the composition of the raise.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Global LNG glut deepens, JKM collapses toward $6, the FY2026 spread/optimization tailwind reverses, marginal recontracting prints sub-$2/MMBtu, and the market re-rates LNG from a premium growth name to a midstream/contracted-infra multiple (~8.5x).
scenarios.narrative_long: The largest supply wave in history (Qatar NFE/NFS + Plaquemines + Golden Pass + Port Arthur + CP2) lands into decelerating demand; JKM-HH compresses to shipping cost with episodic negative merchant margins. The 2026 raise unwinds as optimization/spread upside mean-reverts; Train 7/CCL4 and SPL Expansion FID into the trough at fees at/below legacy. The equity de-rates to ~8.5x EV/EBITDA - the midstream/contracted-infra analog - as the AI/LNG-demand premium deflates.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 8.5x P/E = midstream/contracted-infra band (KMI/EPD historical 8-11x EV/EBITDA-equivalent), below LNG's own forward ~12.3x. Reflects de-rating to a no-growth contracted-infra analog as the supply glut deflates the premium.

eps_bridge.label: FY2026 spread/optimization tailwind reverses + open-book margin normalizes into the glut
eps_bridge.source_tag: S1: LNG 10-K open-position sensitivity (<$50M per $1/MMBtu) + S3 JKM-to-$7 path

eps_bridge.label: Marginal recontracting / new-train fixed fee compresses toward ~$2/MMBtu (at/below legacy)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: Wolfe recontracting assumption; undisclosed marginal fee

eps_bridge.label: Higher rate / slower buyback at depressed FCF (lost per-share accretion)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S2: LNG capital-allocation framework (buyback pace scales with DCF)

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Soft glut: JKM eases toward $7-8, merchant margins normalize, recontracting is delayed rather than punitive, and the multiple compresses from ~10.2x toward ~9x (P/E ~10.5x) - the stock works back toward the sell-side floor / 52-week low.
scenarios.narrative_long: The supply wave pressures spreads but not catastrophically; the contracted book holds FY2027 EBITDA near $7.3B while the premium multiple erodes as base-rate energy-infra de-rating plays out. PT cuts begin (multiple cuts, estimates intact). The stock settles near the $200-220 sell-side floor / $186 52-week low.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 10.5x P/E ~ between the midstream floor and LNG's own forward ~12.3x; consistent with a partial de-rate of the AI/LNG premium with EBITDA intact.

eps_bridge.label: Open-book / spread normalization (JKM toward $7-8)
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: JKM forward curve + LNG open-position sensitivity

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: Contracted book holds, Stage 3 completes end-2026, buyback compounds DCF/share, FY2027 EBITDA lands near $7.8B; the stock holds roughly its current forward multiple (~13x P/E) as the contracted annuity + capital return offset the supply-wave overhang.
scenarios.narrative_long: The ~90%-contracted take-or-pay base delivers FY2027 Consolidated Adj EBITDA ~$7.8B; Stage 3 fully ramps and the >$10B buyback retires ~4-5%/yr of shares, so DCF/share grows even on a flat platform. The supply glut compresses merchant/portfolio margin but the fixed-fee core is insulated; the multiple holds ~13x (in line with the current ~12.3x FY27E forward) rather than re-rating up (capped by oversupply) or down (cushioned by the contracted annuity).
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 13.0x P/E ~ in line with LNG's own current forward (~12.3x FY27E); below VG/CQP-implied and the SRE/KMI utility-midstream band. Holds the multiple rather than re-rating either way.

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: New SPAs + SPL Expansion FID de-risk recontracting at premium fees, buyback accelerates on the raised ~$5B DCF, FY2027 EBITDA tops $8B; the market re-rates LNG toward ~15x as the contracted-growth-plus-capital-return story is recognized.
scenarios.narrative_long: Cheniere signs incremental 15yr+ SPAs at fees at/above legacy and reaches FID on SPL Expansion / Train 7, proving the growth pipeline clears even in oversupply. The raised DCF (~$5B) funds an accelerated buyback toward a ~$30/share run-rate, and the market credits the contracted-compounder narrative with a ~15x multiple (toward the Street's PT).
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 15.0x P/E = re-rate toward the Street PT (~$304.50 ~ 15.6x FY27E), reflecting recognition of the contracted-growth + capital-return compounding; above LNG's current forward but below utility multiples.

eps_bridge.label: Accelerated buyback compounding on raised ~$5B DCF (share count toward ~205M)
New FID accretion begins to price (SPL Expansion / Train 7-CCL4 optionality)

scenarios.narrative_one_liner: AI-power gas-demand supercycle: multiple new premium SPAs + FIDs convert the >100 mtpa permitted platform, JKM re-widens on demand, buyback drives DCF/share toward ~$30, and LNG re-rates to a CQP/utility-adjacent ~17x.
scenarios.narrative_long: Structural gas demand (Asia coal-to-gas + US/global data-center power) outruns even the record supply wave; Cheniere converts a large share of its permitted platform to premium long-dated SPAs and FIDs, the buyback shrinks the share count materially, and the market re-rates the contracted compounder toward ~17x - approaching the utility/contracted-infra premium band.
scenarios.multiple_band_basis: 17.0x P/E approaches a CQP/utility-adjacent premium; tail re-rate predicated on durable premium-fee recontracting + a demand supercycle absorbing the supply wave.

eps_bridge.label: Multiple incremental premium SPAs + FIDs convert the >100 mtpa permitted platform
eps_bridge.source_tag: S3: global LNG demand pipeline (AI-power gas, Asia coal-to-gas)

eps_bridge.label: Aggressive buyback toward ~$30/share run-rate DCF shrinks share count

headline.headline_text: Source-conditional Hold: LNG at $239.40 carries a de-biased 12-month expected return of +0.7%, conditional on the ~90%-contracted book holding its fixed fees and the FY2026 raise proving durable. Anchoring to independent fair value (~$244, ~flat) barely moves it - LNG's DCF was already near spot, so this confirms a genuine (not spot-anchored) Hold; scenario-weighted range -39.6% to +47.0%.

memo_metadata.net_leverage_note: Consolidated net debt ~$21.7B (total debt ~$22.8B less cash ~$1.1B) is from FY2025 XBRL; the EV-implied net debt + non-controlling interest of ~$26.5B (EV $76.6B less mkt cap $50.2B) includes the ~51% public CQP minority and is the figure used in EV/EBITDA. Both are disclosed to avoid conflating consolidated vs EV-level leverage. [v2 DE-BIASED: base re-anchored to independent fair value ~$244; rating CONFIRMED Hold (DCF was already near spot); v1 archived.]

valuation_parallel.method: Two-value parallel: independent fair value (B) || market-regime value (A); the GAP is the mispricing signal; the 12-month base PT is a convergence-weighted blend.
valuation_parallel.independent_fair_value_basis: DCF $225 + SOTP $262 + comps $253 (contracted DCF near spot) — computed WITHOUT reference to price (answers 'what is it worth')
valuation_parallel.market_regime_value_basis: consensus 12-mo PT median/avg (S4) — embeds the current-multiple regime (answers 'where will it trade given the regime')
valuation_parallel.convergence_assumption: no material gap — fair value approx equals spot; the v1 Hold was genuine, not spot-anchored

is_cyclical_note: Not classically cyclical (contracted / fee-based / oligopoly cash flows); valued on contracted DCF / P-DCF / sector multiple rather than a normalized-cycle frame.

conviction_drivers_for: independent fair value vs spot gap is the load-bearing signal (public)
DCF/SOTP/comps triangulate the fair value without reference to price
conviction_drivers_against: the multiple is a judgment that could re-rate
convergence timing is uncertain (overvaluation/undervaluation is not itself a catalyst)
conviction_axis.self_anchoring_check: BIDIRECTIONAL — the anchoring discipline applies to revisions toward EITHER side; use external analysts' PUBLIC evidence, never their conclusions (multiple/PT/rating).
conviction_axis.downgradeable_note: Conviction is tracked separately from the rating; new PUBLIC counter-evidence lowers conviction even when it does not flip the rating. A nonpublic cross-check may nuance conviction personally but is NOT persisted.
